filename: Short presentation vTMAs - For Peter Sorger.pptx
full_text:
In-silico evaluation of tissue microarray performance in ovarian cancer

Background

Lin et al., 2023, Cell

‹#›

Study questions

Can we use TMA sampling to develop biomarkers?
“Hot or cold” - T-cell infiltration as a prognostic biomarker in ovarian cancer (Zhang 2003, Sato 2005)
Spatial interaction of CD8T and Tumor cells as a predictive biomarker for response to immune checkpoint inhibition + PARP inhibitor (Färkkilä et al 2020)

Study questions

Can we use TMA sampling to develop biomarkers?
“Hot or cold” - T-cell infiltration as a prognostic biomarker in ovarian cancer (Zhang 2003, Sato 2005)
Spatial interaction of CD8T and Tumor cells as a predictive biomarker for response to immune checkpoint inhibition + PARP inhibitor (Färkkilä et al 2020)

Can we use TMA sampling for biological discovery?
Increased stromal and immune infiltration in chemo-exposed tumors (Färkkilä et al 2020, Launonen et al unpublished)
Distinct tumor-immune spatial interactions after neoadjuvant chemotherapy (Launonen, unpublished)
Enhanced tumor-immune interactions in BRCA1/2 mutated ovarian cancer (Launonen et al 2022)

What are the conditions of TMA applicability: Disease/histology, tumor grade, tumor (intra sample vs inter-patient) heterogeneity, features, feature size, magnitude of feature difference between groups?

Topacio, 26 samples

22 Finnish (Sciset) samples

Study material: 48 WSI tCyCIF + multi-modal data from two independent ovarian cancer cohorts

1. Core selection guided by Pathologist

2. Random sampling

Study approach

Pathologist selection for cancer rich areas - according to clinical practise (Dr. Virtanen)
Computational approach
- Selection of cancer rich areas
-[d] Maximum possible distance
 between cores [y/n]
-[m] Minimum cells = 1200
-[o] Non-overlapping = True 
-[r]Core radius = 500µm
-[n] N=4

-Minimum cells = 1200 (500?)
-Non-overlapping = False
-Core radius = 500µm
-Random
-N=4

Morphological features?

1 . Concordance to WSI
Exploration of parameters [d, m, o, r] and tumor areas that maximize the concordance with WSI

2. Concordance to biological findings from WSI
Changes in TME composition after chemotherapy
